div.head: The Minister in Honduras (Lay) to the Secretary of State
div.p:
60. The following telegram was sent to Nicaragua, July 14, 9 p.m.:
“Your July 9, 3 p.m., to the Department. The Minister for Foreign Affairs informs me that Honduran Government would have no objection to the additional provision mentioned in your telegram. He intimated that the President desires that the protocol be signed at an early date and in Tegucigalpa. Would the Government of Nicaragua be disposed to accept an invitation direct from Honduras to sign the protocol at Tegucigalpa? If so, at the appropriate time I can suggest to President that he extend such an invitation. An invitation was extended to Honduras to sign a similar protocol here (see your telegram of November 8, 3 p.m., 1929, to the DepartmentForeign Relations, 1929, vol. i, p. 983.).”
